note: Bumped 2026-05-28 from 282 KB → 300 KB to absorb @modelcontextprotocol/ext-apps@^1.7.2 adoption (Phase 1.19b.2) — iframe-runtime's host-context-emitter now applies host theme + style variables + fonts to the iframe DOM via the spec-canonical `applyDocumentTheme` / `applyHostStyleVariables` / `applyHostFonts` helpers. ext-apps's root `.` export bundles the App class + zod-v4 eventSchemas (the package doesn't ship a `./styles` subpath), so importing the three styles helpers pulls ~15 KB gz of App-class machinery the iframe-runtime doesn't directly use today. Acceptable cost — eliminates the drift class around the protocol's `host-context-changed` notification shape, and the App class itself is a future replacement candidate for the hand-rolled JSON-RPC postMessage layer in runtime.ts (Phase 1.19b.3, deferred). Prior bump 2026-05-26 (281 KB → 282 KB): spec-canonical postMessage resolver chain in `bootSequence` (#143) — bootSequence now races a `ui/notifications/tool-result` listener (MCP-Apps SEP-1865) against synchronous Reading-B + inline-global tiers, and the autostart layer threads pre-resolved postMessage meta through `runBootProduction(preResolvedMeta)` so spec-strict hosts (`<AppRenderer>` from `@mcp-ui/client`, ChatGPT MCP-Apps connector) don't re-await the same notification inside bootSequence. Prior bump 2026-05-23 (280 KB → 281 KB): @ggui-ai/wire's new dispatch-dedup module (~150 bytes gz) — the task-scoped duplicate-dispatch defense in `useAction` that closes the scenario-7 double-fire bug class. Prior bump 2026-05-14 (228 KB → 280 KB): absorbed Ajv 8 + ajv-formats (~31 KB gz) for the closed-shape validator the iframe runs symmetrically with the server. Load-bearing contributors: React 19 ~115 KB, zod + JSON-Schema processors ~60 KB, protocol root-barrel leak ~15 KB, Ajv ~31 KB, design + wire ~60 KB, ext-apps App + zod-v4 ~15 KB. Future tightening options: upstream PR for ext-apps `./styles` subpath export (decouples helpers from the App class); subpath-export contract-validator behind `@ggui-ai/protocol/validation` so iframe can opt-in to a slim observer-only path; tree-shake openrouter-models behind a subpath.
